Within the plant kingdom, Leandra clidemioides is classified as a flowering plant, specifically belonging to the class Magnoliopsida and the order Myrtales. intro: Leandra clidemioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known as the "erva-baleeira" in Portuguese, native to the Atlantic coastal forests of Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which are beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Leandra clidemioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leandra clidemioides is often prepared as a tea or infusion, known as "chá de erva-baleeira", or as a tincture, to harness its health benefits and therapeutic properties.
